text: The surgeon uses a Keith needle with number one nylon to retract the dissected mesocolon during a laparoscopic procedure.

The surgeon threads a needle through the anterior abdominal wall, keeping the thread lax, and uses a Hemolock applicator to secure the mesocolon for retraction. The Hemolock is applied to the peritoneum, and the needle is brought back through the abdominal wall in a retrograde fashion. The procedure is repeated on the left side, identifying optimal retraction points.
The surgeon uses a Hemolock device to securely retract the peritoneum and mesocolon tissue, employing a straight needle for effective retrograde retraction.

The surgeon demonstrates effective retraction of the mesocolon using a Keith needle and Hemolock, showcasing the technique's ability to hold tissue securely without concern for major vascular structures. The retraction is visibly well-executed, highlighting its potential application in other areas of the abdominal cavity, including the uterus.
